MAK0WIEC ZE ŚLIWKAMI

Makowiec is a traditional Polish poppy seed cake, and this version adds the
sweetness and texture of plums (śliwkami). Dense with ground poppy seeds and
studded with dried plums, this cake pairs beautifully w1th coffee or tea.

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 cup poppy seed filling (or grind 3/4 cup poppy seeds with 1/4 cup milk)
- 3/4 cup dried plums, chopped
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup melted butter
- 2 eggs, beaten
- 1/2 cup sour cream
- 1 tsp baking powder
- 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
- Pinch of salt
- 2 tbsp plum jam (optional, for topping)

INSTRUCT|ONS:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Butter and flour a loaf pan.
2. Cream together sugar and melted butter in bowl.
3. Beat in eggs one at a time, then add vanilla extract.
4. Fold in poppy seed filling and sour cream alternately with flour mixture.
5. Gently stir in chopped p1ums and baking powder.
6. Pour batter into prepared pan.
7. Bake 45-50 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
8. Cool in pan 15 minutes before turning out onto wire rack.
9. Optional: Brush warm cake with plum jam for extra sweetness.

SERVÍNG AND STORAGE:
Best served sliced, with strong coffee or tea. Keeps well wrapped at room
temperature for 3 days. May also be frozen for up to 1 month in airtight container.
Serve at room temperature or warmed slightly. Flavors deepen after 1-2 days.
